summary: Ukrainian forces reported guided aviation bomb strikes on Zaporizhzhia and Kharkiv regions, while Russian forces claimed liberation of Bogodarovka in Dnipropetrovsk. Multiple Shahed and strike UAVs operated across Poltava, Kremenchuk, Bohodukhiv, and the Dnipropetrovsk‑Kharkiv border areas. Bridges in occupied Donetsk and Luhansk were hit, and Russian drone attacks damaged energy facilities in Zaporizhzhia and caused civilian injuries in Bryansk and Belgorod.
key_points: Guided aviation bombs were launched on the city of Zaporizhzhia (Source: @kpszsu/66829).
Guided aviation bombs were also deployed over Kharkiv region (Source: @kpszsu/66826).
Ukrainian sources reported nine Shahed drones hitting Poltava, one hitting Kremenchuk, and three from Kharkiv targeting Poltava (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68162).
A group of strike UAVs operated east of Bohodukhiv in Kharkiv region, heading south (Source: @kpszsu/66836).
Ukrainian forces said they hit three important bridges in occupied Donetsk (automobile bridge near Novoazovsk) and two railway bridges in Luhansk (Source: @UkraineNow/82268).
Russian Defense Ministry announced the liberation of Bogodarovka in Dnipropetrovsk region (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31243).
A Russian kamikaze drone struck a bus in Petryatinka village, Bryansk region, wounding a woman (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
Russian FPV drone attacks on a commercial facility in Proletarsky, Belgorod region caused barotrauma to a woman (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).

sections.executive_summary: Guided aviation bomb strikes hit Zaporizhzhia and Kharkiv regions, indicating intensified Russian air operations. Ukrainian forces reported extensive Shahed and strike UAV activity across Poltava, Kremenchuk, and border zones, while also damaging key bridges in occupied Donetsk and Luhansk. Russia announced the recapture of Bogodarovka and continued drone attacks on civilian targets in Bryansk and Belgorod.

points: Ukrainian defenses reported nine Shahed drones striking Poltava, one hitting Kremenchuk, and three Shahed drones launched from Kharkiv toward Poltava, demonstrating a concentrated UAV offensive in central Ukraine (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68162).

Ukrainian sources claimed the destruction of an automobile bridge near Novoazovsk in Donetsk and two railway bridges in Luhansk, disrupting Russian logistics across occupied territories (Source: @UkraineNow/82268).
Ukrainian forces reported a group of strike UAVs operating east of Bohodukhiv in Kharkiv region, moving southward, suggesting targeting of rear areas (Source: @kpszsu/66836).
Ukrainian intelligence noted a UAV group north of Pavlograd on the Dnipropetrovsk‑Kharkiv border heading toward Poltava, indicating cross‑border aerial pressure (Source: @kpszsu/66831).
Ukrainian reports indicated a UAV group southeast of Poltava moving south, and another group southeast of Poltava heading south, showing multiple vectors of UAV attacks (Source: @kpszsu/66825).
Ukrainian forces claimed to have destroyed a bridge over the Senek River with a FAB strike to hinder Russian logistics (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
Ukrainian statements highlighted ongoing drone strikes on Kharkov, Sumy, Pavlograd, and Mykolaiv regions, reflecting a broad aerial campaign (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
Ukrainian forces reported guided aviation bomb deployments over Kharkiv region, indicating exposure to Russian precision strike assets (Source: @kpszsu/66826).
Russian Defense Ministry announced the liberation of Bogodarovka in Dnipropetrovsk region, expanding Russian control eastward (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31243).
A Russian kamikaze drone struck a civilian bus in Petryatinka village, Bryansk region, wounding a woman, showing use of low‑cost suicide UAVs against soft targets (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).

An FPV drone attacked a commercial facility in Proletarsky, Belgorod region, causing barotrauma to a woman, indicating continued Russian drone harassment of border settlements (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).

Russian forces reported damage to energy facilities in Zaporizhzhia region, causing widespread power outages across the region and in Kherson, highlighting strategic targeting of infrastructure (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
The Crimean Bridge was closed following an incident, reflecting heightened security concerns over critical transport links (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).

Russian reports indicated intensive drone strikes on Ukrainian territory, including Kharkov, Sumy, Pavlograd, and Mykolaiv, demonstrating a sustained aerial pressure campaign (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
Russian statements claimed that Ukrainian drone downings in NATO states are an acceptable cost, underscoring the broader geopolitical dimension of the UAV campaign (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31245).
Russian forces continued assault operations in urban areas of Krasny Liman and Konstantinovka, facing Ukrainian drone retaliation (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
Ukrainian forces reported guided aviation bomb strikes on Kharkiv region, indicating Russian air activity on the eastern front (Source: @kpszsu/66826).
UAV groups operating east of Bohodukhiv and north of Pavlograd moved southward toward Poltava, suggesting pressure on supply routes behind the front line (Source: @kpszsu/66836; @kpszsu/66831).

Russian reports of drone strikes on Kharkov and Sumy districts, including small‑arms battles in Bachevsk and surrounding villages, show ongoing contested ground engagements (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
Ukrainian claim of destroying a bridge over the Senek River with a FAB strike aims to disrupt Russian logistics on the eastern axis (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
Russian forces liberated Bogodarovka in Dnipropetrovsk, extending their foothold eastward (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31243).
Ukrainian reports of UAV activity in the Vovchansk sector and fighting in villages such as Losevka and Ukrainske indicate continued frontline volatility (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
Russian assault operations continued in urban areas of Krasny Liman and Konstantinovka, facing Ukrainian drone counter‑attacks (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
Ukrainian forces reported a bridge over the Senek River destroyed, impacting Russian supply lines on the eastern front (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
UAV groups were observed north of Dnipropetrovsk region in Pereshchepne heading west, indicating aerial activity near the northern front (Source: @kpszsu/66833).
UAVs were reported north of Pavlograd on the Dnipropetrovsk‑Kharkiv border moving toward Poltava, suggesting pressure on northern supply corridors (Source: @kpszsu/66831).
In Chernihiv region, at least three UAVs were detected along the border near Dobryanka, highlighting border‑area aerial surveillance (Source: @eRadarrua/98707).
Ukrainian reports noted a UAV strike on a commercial facility in Proletarsky, Belgorod region, close to the northern Ukrainian border (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
Russian drone activity in Bryansk region, including a kamikaze strike on a bus in Petryatinka, reflects cross‑border UAV operations affecting the northern theater (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
Ukrainian forces reported strike UAVs north of Pavlograd heading toward Poltava, indicating potential threat to northern logistics (Source: @kpszsu/66831).
Ukrainian intelligence noted UAV groups operating in the north‑west direction from Pereshchepine, possibly targeting rear areas (Source: @kpszsu/66833).
Russian statements about NATO escalation suggest heightened strategic focus on the northern front (Source: @tass_agency/382963).
Guided aviation bombs were launched on Zaporizhzhia city, a key southern industrial hub, indicating intensified Russian air strikes (Source: @kpszsu/66829).
Russian attacks damaged energy facilities in Zaporizhzhia region, causing widespread outages and affecting civilian and military power supplies (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).

Ukrainian forces reported bridge damage in occupied Donetsk (automobile bridge near Novoazovsk) and two railway bridges in Luhansk, impacting Russian logistics in the south (Source: @UkraineNow/82268).
Ukrainian reports of UAV activity southeast of Poltava heading south suggest pressure on routes toward the southern front (Source: @kpszsu/66825).
Russian drone strikes were reported on Kherson region, leading to power outages across the area (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
The Crimean Bridge closure after an incident underscores vulnerability of critical southern transport links (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
Ukrainian claims of destroying a bridge over the Senek River aim to hinder Russian supply lines feeding the southern front (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
Russian forces continued assault operations in urban areas of Krasny Liman and Konstantinovka, affecting southern supply corridors (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
Russian guided aviation bomb deployments hit Zaporizhzhia and Kharkiv regions, showing use of precision air assets against urban centers (Source: @kpszsu/66829; @kpszsu/66826).
Ukrainian reports listed multiple Shahed drone attacks on Poltava and Kremenchuk, indicating a sustained UAV barrage across central Ukraine (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68162).

Strike UAV groups operated east of Bohodukhiv, southeast of Poltava, and north of Pavlograd, moving southward, reflecting coordinated aerial harassment of rear areas (Source: @kpszsu/66836; @kpszsu/66825; @kpszsu/66831).
Russian kamikaze drone attacks on civilian buses in Bryansk and commercial facilities in Belgorod demonstrate low‑cost suicide UAV tactics near the border (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
Ukrainian forces reported UAV activity north of Dnipropetrovsk in Pereshchepne heading west, suggesting reconnaissance or strike missions on the northern flank (Source: @kpszsu/66833).
Russian drone strikes targeted energy infrastructure in Zaporizhzhia, causing regional power outages and affecting both civilian and military grids (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
Ukrainian claims of destroying a bridge over the Senek River with a FAB strike indicate integration of air‑delivered munitions in ground operations (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
The closure of the Crimean Bridge after an incident points to aerial threats against critical maritime infrastructure (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).

key_developments: Guided aviation bombs struck Zaporizhzhia city, indicating intensified Russian air operations against a major industrial center (Source: @kpszsu/66829).
Nine Shahed drones hit Poltava and one hit Kremenchuk, showing a concentrated UAV offensive in central Ukraine (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68162).
Russian forces announced the liberation of Bogodarovka in Dnipropetrovsk region, expanding their territorial control (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31243).
Ukrainian forces reported destruction of an automobile bridge near Novoazovsk and two railway bridges in Luhansk, disrupting Russian logistics (Source: @UkraineNow/82268).
A Russian kamikaze drone struck a bus in Petryatinka, Bryansk region, wounding a civilian, highlighting the use of suicide UAVs near the border (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
Energy facilities in Zaporizhzhia were damaged, causing widespread power outages across the region and in Kherson (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
The Crimean Bridge was closed following an incident, affecting a critical supply route for Russian forces (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian air assets are employing guided bombs against key Ukrainian cities while expanding UAV operations across central and southern fronts, targeting infrastructure and logistics. Ukrainian UAV activity remains high, with Shahed attacks on Poltava and coordinated strike UAV groups threatening rear areas. Russian use of kamikaze drones near border regions adds a low‑cost, high‑impact threat to civilian targets, while bridge destructions indicate a focus on disrupting supply lines. The closure of the Crimean Bridge underscores vulnerability of strategic transport corridors.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian authorities have prioritized bridge repairs and reinforced air defenses around Zaporizhzhia and Kharkiv to mitigate guided bomb impacts. Energy operators are managing load increases due to heat and repairing damaged facilities. Russian statements emphasize continued pressure on NATO and the use of UAVs to sustain offensive momentum. Local administrations in occupied Donetsk and Luhansk report logistical challenges following bridge losses, while border regions increase vigilance against cross‑border drone incursions.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of UAV launch sites near Bohodukhiv, Poltava, and Pereshchepne is critical for early warning of strike vectors. The pattern of guided bomb deployments suggests a shift toward targeting urban industrial hubs. Russian liberation of Bogodarovka may serve as a staging point for further eastward advances. The use of kamikaze drones near Bryansk and Belgorod indicates a diversification of attack methods that could spill over into Ukrainian airspace. Continued assessment of bridge damage effects on Russian supply chains is essential.
